new_value: {"notes":"COMPLETED: Decided on 100ms debounce interval for PTY resize events. Rationale: (1) below 150ms human perception threshold so feels responsive, (2) matches industry standard (Alacritty, iTerm2, WezTerm all use 100-200ms), (3) eliminates resize storms at typical 60-120Hz drag event rates, (4) particularly beneficial for Windows ConPTY which has higher per-resize overhead. Documented in SPEC.md under Terminal resize section with pseudocode implementation pattern."}

{"id":"dispatch-394","title":"Simplify orchestration: remove planner, single Dispatcher handles all routing","description":"Simplify the app flow by removing the separate planner process. The orchestrator (Dispatcher) should handle everything: receive messages, decide what to do, break down tasks itself, and dispatch agents directly.

Current flow: App > Voice > Text > Orchestrator > Planner > Agent
Target flow: App > Voice > Text > Orchestrator (Dispatcher) > Agent

The orchestrator should:
- Receive the message and figure out what to do
- If the user directly addresses an agent (e.g. 'Bravo, fix the login bug'), dispatch directly to that agent
- If the task is complex, break it down itself and dispatch multiple agents
- No separate planner subprocess needed","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"50547619+craigrusselltiu@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T06:23:37Z","created_by":"Craig Russell Tiu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T06:23:37Z"}

{"id":"dispatch-1we","title":"Bidirectional agent-orchestrator communication","description":"Enable agents to talk back to the orchestrator (Dispatcher), creating a two-way communication channel.

Currently: Orchestrator can message agents via message_agent tool, but agents have no way to reply. Task completion is detected passively by the console via idle detection.

Target: Agents can send messages back to the Dispatcher by outputting a specific marker pattern in their terminal. The console detects this pattern in the vt100 parser output and relays it to the orchestrator.

Example conversation:
  Dispatcher -> Alpha: 'Fix the login bug in auth.rs'
  Alpha works, hits a question
  Alpha outputs: [TO DISPATCH] auth.rs has two login functions - handle_login and login_v2. Which one?
  Console relays: [MSG] Alpha: auth.rs has two login functions...
  Dispatcher -> Alpha: 'Fix handle_login, login_v2 is deprecated'
  Alpha continues working","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"50547619+craigrusselltiu@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T06:35:27Z","created_by":"Craig Russell Tiu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T06:35:27Z"}
